meta.title: MetaLens AI — Free AI-Powered Medical Meta-Analysis Tool
meta.description: Enter medical keywords and get instant AI-powered meta-analysis summaries from 40M+ PubMed papers. Free tool by SPINAI.

hero.title: AI-Powered Medical Meta-Analysis

tools.journalDisclaimer: Impact factors are approximate. Verify current IF and submission guidelines on the journal's official website.

about.title: About MetaLens AI
about.missionText:
MetaLens AI was created by SPINAI with a clear and ambitious mission: to democratize access to medical research insights for healthcare professionals, students, and researchers worldwide. We believe that the ability to quickly synthesize evidence from thousands of peer-reviewed papers should not be limited by expensive subscription fees or the time-consuming process of manual literature reviews.

Every day, groundbreaking medical research is published across thousands of journals. Yet accessing and synthesizing this knowledge remains a significant barrier for many healthcare professionals, especially those in resource-limited settings or non-English-speaking countries. MetaLens AI bridges this gap by providing instant, AI-powered meta-analysis summaries from PubMed's database of over 40 million biomedical papers — completely free of charge.
about.whyText:
Healthcare professionals face a growing challenge: the volume of medical literature doubles approximately every 73 days, making it virtually impossible to stay current with all relevant research. A single systematic review can take 6-18 months and cost thousands of dollars. Existing AI research tools like Elicit ($12-49/month), Consensus ($8.99/month), and SciSpace ($20/month) help but remain costly for students and professionals in developing countries.

MetaLens AI solves this problem by providing:

• Instant Analysis: Enter keywords and receive a structured summary in under 30 seconds.
• Zero Cost: Our free tier requires no account, no API key, and no payment.
• Multi-Language Support: Automatic UI translation and AI results in 8 languages.
• Evidence-Based: Every finding is backed by cited PubMed papers with direct links.
• Accessible: Works on any device with a web browser — no installation required.
about.howWeWorkText:
MetaLens AI combines cutting-edge artificial intelligence with the world's most comprehensive biomedical database to deliver research insights in seconds. Here's how our technology pipeline works:

1. Keyword Processing: Your medical keywords are processed and optimized for PubMed search. Non-English keywords are automatically translated to English for maximum search coverage.

2. PubMed Search: We query the National Library of Medicine's E-utilities API, searching across 40+ million indexed papers from over 30,000 biomedical journals.

3. Abstract Retrieval: Relevant paper abstracts, along with metadata (authors, journal, year, PMID), are retrieved and prepared for AI analysis.

4. AI Synthesis: Our AI engine analyzes the abstracts and synthesizes findings into a structured meta-analysis-style summary covering key comparisons, statistics, recommendations, caveats, and limitations.

5. Multilingual Output: For non-English users, the summary is intelligently condensed and translated into their language — not a word-for-word translation, but a natural, concise summary.

6. Citation Linking: Every finding includes PMID references linked directly to the original papers on PubMed for verification.
about.transparencyText:
We believe in complete transparency about what MetaLens AI can and cannot do:

What We Do Well:
• Rapidly synthesize key findings from multiple research abstracts
• Provide structured, citation-backed summaries for preliminary literature reviews
• Make medical research accessible in multiple languages
• Save hours of manual literature review work

Our Limitations:
• We analyze abstracts only, not full-text papers — important methodological details may be missed
• AI can make errors, misinterpret findings, or produce biased conclusions
• This is NOT a replacement for formal systematic reviews or meta-analyses
• Results should always be verified against original source papers
• The tool supplements but never replaces expert medical judgment

We use hedging language ("suggests," "appears to," "evidence indicates") throughout our summaries and always link to original sources so you can verify every finding.
about.teamText:
SPINAI is a technology company that builds AI-powered tools for medical and research professionals. Our goal is to make complex medical literature analysis fast and accessible for everyone.

Our team combines expertise in biomedical informatics, natural language processing, and user experience design to create tools that are both powerful and intuitive. MetaLens AI is our flagship product, and we aim to be the best AI analysis tool for researchers and healthcare professionals.
about.openText:
MetaLens AI is built on a modern, reliable technology stack:

• PubMed E-utilities API: Access to 40M+ biomedical papers from the National Library of Medicine
• Advanced AI Synthesis: State-of-the-art language models for accurate research analysis
• Vercel Edge Network: Fast, reliable global hosting with automatic SSL and CDN
• Modern Stack: Built with Next.js, TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS

No API keys required for users. Simple sign-up. Generous access for all researchers and students.
about.contactText: We value your feedback and are always looking to improve MetaLens AI. Whether you have questions, suggestions, bug reports, or collaboration ideas, we would love to hear from you. Reach out at

howItWorks.title: How It Works
howItWorks.step1Text: Type in medical terms, drug names, conditions, or any research keywords you want to investigate.
howItWorks.step2Text: Our system searches PubMed's 40M+ papers to find the most relevant studies for your query.
howItWorks.step3Text: Advanced AI reads the abstracts and synthesizes a structured meta-analysis summary with key findings.
howItWorks.step4Text: Receive a concise summary with source citations and links to the original papers.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Find answers to the most common questions about MetaLens AI, our AI-powered medical meta-analysis tool.
faq.q1: Is MetaLens AI completely free to use?
faq.a1: Yes! MetaLens AI offers a generous free tier that allows up to 5 analyses per day. The free tier includes structured 3-point summaries, PubMed search across 40M+ papers, multilingual support, and direct links to source papers. For power users, we offer Pro ($2.99/mo or $29.99/yr) with 50 analyses/day and 7-point summaries, and Ultra ($6.99/mo or $69.99/yr) with 200 analyses/day and 10-point summaries plus advanced features.
faq.q2: Where does MetaLens AI get its research data?
faq.a2: All research data comes exclusively from PubMed (https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/), the world's largest and most trusted biomedical literature database. PubMed is maintained by the National Center for Biotechnology Information (NCBI) at the U.S. National Library of Medicine (NLM), part of the National Institutes of Health (NIH). The database contains over 40 million citations and abstracts from biomedical literature, covering medicine, nursing, dentistry, veterinary medicine, healthcare systems, and preclinical sciences.
faq.q3: Is the information provided by MetaLens AI considered medical advice?
faq.a3: No, absolutely not. MetaLens AI provides AI-generated research summaries for informational and educational purposes only. The summaries are not a substitute for professional medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment. Always consult a qualified healthcare professional before making any medical decisions. The tool is designed to assist with preliminary literature reviews and research exploration, not to guide clinical decisions.
faq.q4: How accurate are the AI-generated results?
faq.a4: MetaLens AI synthesizes findings directly from peer-reviewed research abstracts indexed in PubMed. While we strive for the highest possible accuracy, it's important to understand that AI can occasionally make errors, misinterpret findings, or miss nuances present in the full-text papers. We use hedging language throughout our summaries and always provide direct links to the original PubMed papers so you can verify every finding. We recommend treating MetaLens AI results as a starting point for your research, not as definitive conclusions.
faq.q5: What languages does MetaLens AI support?
faq.a5: MetaLens AI supports 8 languages: English, Korean (한국어), Japanese (日本語), Chinese (中文), Spanish (Español), Portuguese (Português), German (Deutsch), and French (Français). The user interface automatically detects your browser's language settings and displays in your preferred language. AI-generated summaries are also provided in your language as concise, localized summaries — not word-for-word translations.
faq.q6: How does the AI synthesis work?
faq.a6: When you enter keywords, MetaLens AI follows a multi-step process: (1) Your keywords are used to search PubMed's database via their E-utilities API, (2) Relevant paper abstracts and metadata are retrieved, (3) Our AI engine (powered by Google Gemini) reads and analyzes the abstracts, (4) The AI synthesizes a structured summary covering key comparisons, quantitative findings, recommendations, exceptions, and limitations, (5) For non-English users, the summary is condensed into a natural-language summary in their language. The entire process typically takes 15-30 seconds.
faq.q7: Can I use MetaLens AI for academic research or publications?
faq.a7: MetaLens AI is an excellent tool for preliminary literature exploration and getting a quick overview of research on a topic. However, it should not be cited as a primary source in academic publications. Instead, use it to identify relevant papers and then cite the original research articles directly. If you reference MetaLens AI in your workflow, please acknowledge it as an AI-assisted research tool and always verify findings against the original PubMed sources.
faq.q8: What types of medical research can I analyze?
faq.a8: MetaLens AI can analyze any topic covered by PubMed's database, which includes virtually all areas of biomedical and health research: drug comparisons and efficacy studies, disease mechanisms and treatments, clinical trials and outcomes research, pharmacology and toxicology, public health and epidemiology, surgical techniques and outcomes, mental health and psychiatry, nutrition and dietary interventions, and much more. The best results come from specific, well-defined queries comparing treatments, drugs, or interventions.
faq.q9: Is my search data private? Do you store my queries?
faq.a9: We take your privacy seriously. We collect minimal anonymous usage data (search keywords, browser language, paper count) for service improvement purposes only. We do NOT collect personally identifiable information such as names, email addresses, or IP addresses. Search queries are processed in real-time and are not persistently stored on our servers. For full details, please review our Privacy Policy.
faq.q10: What should I do if the results seem inaccurate or incomplete?
faq.a10: If you receive results that seem inaccurate or incomplete, try the following: (1) Refine your keywords — more specific terms tend to produce better results, (2) Try different keyword combinations to capture different aspects of your research question, (3) Always click through to the original PubMed papers to verify the AI's interpretation, (4) Remember that the AI analyzes abstracts only, so full-text details may not be reflected. If you encounter persistent issues, please use the feedback link to report the problem and help us improve the service.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: MetaLens AI, operated by SPINAI, is committed to protecting your privacy. This comprehensive Privacy Policy explains in detail what information we collect, how we use it, how we protect it, and what rights you have regarding your data. By using MetaLens AI, you agree to the practices described in this policy. We encourage you to read this document carefully to understand our approach to data handling.
privacy.s1Text:
MetaLens AI is designed with a privacy-first approach. We collect only the minimum amount of data necessary to operate and improve our service. Specifically, we collect:

• Search Keywords: The medical terms and keywords you enter to perform analyses. These are used solely to query the PubMed database and generate AI-powered summaries.
• Browser Language: Your browser's language setting is detected automatically to provide the user interface and AI results in your preferred language.
• Paper Count: The number of PubMed papers returned for your query, used for aggregate analytics to understand query effectiveness.
• Session Identifier: A randomly generated UUID stored in your browser's session storage. This identifier is not linked to any personally identifiable information and expires when you close your browser.
• User Agent: Your browser's user agent string, collected for compatibility analytics and debugging purposes.
• Referrer URL: The website that referred you to MetaLens AI, used to understand traffic sources.

Importantly, we do NOT collect: names, email addresses, phone numbers, physical addresses, IP addresses (we do not log them), payment information, or any other personally identifiable information (PII).
privacy.s2Text:
The information we collect is used exclusively for the following purposes:

• Service Delivery: Your search keywords are sent to PubMed's E-utilities API to retrieve relevant research papers, and then to our AI synthesis engine to generate structured meta-analysis summaries.
• Service Improvement: Aggregated, anonymous usage data helps us understand how users interact with MetaLens AI, which types of queries are most common, and where we can improve the user experience.
• Technical Maintenance: Browser and session data helps us identify and resolve technical issues, ensure cross-browser compatibility, and maintain service reliability.
• Analytics: We use anonymous analytics to track overall usage patterns, popular search topics, and service performance metrics. This data is never used to identify individual users.

We do NOT use your data for: targeted advertising, user profiling, selling to third parties, or any purpose other than operating and improving MetaLens AI.
privacy.s3Text:
MetaLens AI uses minimal cookie and tracking technologies:

• Session Storage: We use browser session storage (not persistent cookies) to store a random session identifier. This expires when you close your browser tab.
• Google AdSense: Our website displays advertisements served by Google AdSense. Google AdSense may use cookies and web beacons to serve ads based on your prior visits to our website or other websites on the internet. Google's use of advertising cookies enables it and its partners to serve ads based on your visit to MetaLens AI and/or other sites on the Internet. You may opt out of personalized advertising by visiting Google's Ads Settings (https://www.google.com/settings/ads) or by visiting the Network Advertising Initiative opt-out page (https://www.networkadvertising.org/choices/).
• Google Analytics: We may use Google Analytics to collect anonymous usage statistics. Google Analytics uses cookies to track user interactions. The information generated by the cookie about your use of the website is transmitted to and stored by Google. You can opt out of Google Analytics by installing the Google Analytics Opt-out Browser Add-on.
• No Fingerprinting: We do not use browser fingerprinting, supercookies, or any other invasive tracking technology.

You can control cookie settings through your browser preferences. Disabling cookies may affect the display of advertisements but will not impact the core functionality of MetaLens AI.
privacy.s4Text:
MetaLens AI integrates with the following third-party services to deliver its functionality. Each service is governed by its own privacy policy, and we encourage you to review them:

• PubMed / NCBI / NIH (National Institutes of Health): We use PubMed's free E-utilities API to search and retrieve biomedical literature data. PubMed is operated by the U.S. National Library of Medicine. Privacy policy: https://www.nlm.nih.gov/web_policies.html
• Google Gemini AI: We use Google's Gemini API for AI-powered text synthesis and analysis. Your search queries and retrieved abstracts are sent to Google's servers for processing. Google's privacy policy: https://policies.google.com/privacy
• Google Gemini AI: As a fallback AI provider, we may send data to Google Gemini AI for text synthesis when the primary AI service is unavailable.
• Google AdSense: We use Google AdSense to display advertisements. AdSense may collect data through cookies as described in Section 3 above. Google AdSense privacy: https://policies.google.com/technologies/ads
• Google Analytics: We may use Google Analytics for anonymous traffic analysis. Google Analytics privacy: https://policies.google.com/privacy
• Google Sheets (via Apps Script): Anonymous, aggregated usage statistics may be sent to Google Sheets for analytics purposes. No personally identifiable information is included in this data.
• Vercel: Our website is hosted on Vercel's edge network. Vercel may process server logs as part of hosting operations. Vercel privacy: https://vercel.com/legal/privacy-policy
privacy.s5Text:
We follow strict data retention practices:

• Search Queries: Your search keywords are processed in real-time and are not persistently stored on our servers after the analysis is complete.
• Anonymous Analytics: Aggregated usage statistics (keyword trends, paper counts, language distribution) are retained for up to 12 months for product improvement purposes and then permanently deleted.
• Session Data: Session identifiers expire when you close your browser and are never stored server-side.
• No Personal Data Retention: Since we do not collect personally identifiable information, there is no personal data to retain or delete.
• Third-Party Retention: Data sent to third-party services (PubMed, Google, Google Gemini AI) is subject to those services' respective data retention policies.
privacy.s6Title: 6. Your Rights Under GDPR, CCPA, and Other Regulations
privacy.s6Text:
We respect the privacy rights of all users worldwide. Depending on your jurisdiction, you may have the following rights:

• Right to Access: You have the right to know what data we collect about you. As stated above, we do not collect personally identifiable information.
• Right to Deletion: You may request deletion of any data associated with your use. Since we do not collect PII, there is no personal data to delete.
• Right to Opt Out: You may opt out of Google AdSense personalized advertising and Google Analytics tracking as described in Section 3.
• Right to Non-Discrimination: We will not discriminate against you for exercising any of your privacy rights.
• Right to Data Portability: Since no personal data is collected, this right is not applicable.

For users in the European Economic Area (EEA): We process data based on legitimate interest (service operation and improvement). We do not transfer personal data outside the EEA because we do not collect personal data.

For California residents (CCPA/CPRA): We do not sell, share, or use personal information for cross-context behavioral advertising. We do not collect sensitive personal information.

To exercise any of these rights or for any privacy-related inquiries, please contact us at the email address provided below.
privacy.s7Text: MetaLens AI is not a covered entity or business associate under the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA). This tool exclusively analyzes publicly available research abstracts from the PubMed database. It does not process, store, transmit, or have access to any protected health information (PHI), electronic health records (EHR), patient data, or clinical information. Users should never input personal health information, patient identifiers, or any protected data into MetaLens AI.
privacy.s8Text: MetaLens AI is designed for healthcare professionals, medical students, researchers, and adults interested in medical research. This service is not directed at children under the age of 13 (or 16 in the European Economic Area). We do not knowingly collect information from children. If you believe that a child under the applicable age has used our service, please contact us and we will take appropriate steps to address the situation.
privacy.s9Text:
We implement appropriate technical and organizational measures to protect the data we process:

• Encryption in Transit: All data transmitted between your browser and our servers is encrypted using HTTPS/TLS protocols.
• Secure Hosting: Our application is hosted on Vercel's enterprise-grade infrastructure with built-in DDoS protection, automatic HTTPS, and edge security.
• Minimal Data Collection: Our strongest security measure is collecting the absolute minimum amount of data necessary — no PII means no PII can be breached.
• Regular Updates: We keep all software dependencies updated to address known security vulnerabilities.
• API Security: Server-side API keys are stored as encrypted environment variables and are never exposed to the client-side.
privacy.s10Text: MetaLens AI is operated from servers located in various regions through Vercel's global edge network. When you use our service, your search queries may be processed by servers in different countries. Since we do not collect personally identifiable information, international data transfer regulations (such as GDPR Chapter V) regarding personal data do not apply to the anonymous, non-personal data we process. However, we ensure that all third-party services we integrate with maintain appropriate data protection standards.
privacy.s11Text: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time to reflect changes in our practices, technology, legal requirements, or other factors. When we make material changes, we will update the "Last updated" date at the top of this page. We encourage you to review this Privacy Policy periodically to stay informed about how we protect your information. Your continued use of MetaLens AI after any changes to this Privacy Policy constitutes your acceptance of those changes.
privacy.s12Text: If you have any questions, concerns, or requests regarding this Privacy Policy or our data practices, please contact us at:

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.intro: Welcome to MetaLens AI, a service operated by SPINAI. By accessing or using MetaLens AI (the "Service"), you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service ("Terms"). If you do not agree to these Terms, you may not use the Service. Please read these Terms carefully before using MetaLens AI.
terms.s1Text: MetaLens AI is a free online tool that provides AI-generated summaries of publicly available medical research abstracts from PubMed, the world's largest biomedical literature database maintained by the U.S. National Library of Medicine. The Service searches PubMed's database of over 40 million papers based on user-provided keywords, retrieves relevant abstracts, and uses artificial intelligence to synthesize structured meta-analysis-style summaries. The Service is designed to assist healthcare professionals, medical students, researchers, pharmacists, and clinicians in preliminary literature reviews and evidence-based research.
terms.s2Text:
IMPORTANT: MetaLens AI is NOT a medical device, diagnostic tool, or clinical decision support system. Nothing on this website constitutes medical advice, diagnosis, treatment recommendation, or professional healthcare guidance.

The AI-generated summaries provided by MetaLens AI are for informational and educational purposes only. They are based on publicly available research abstracts and may not reflect the complete findings of the underlying studies. Always consult a qualified healthcare professional before making any medical decisions. Never disregard professional medical advice or delay seeking medical treatment based on information obtained from MetaLens AI.

SPINAI, its affiliates, employees, and agents make no representations or warranties regarding the accuracy, completeness, or reliability of any information provided through the Service.
terms.s3Text:
While we strive for accuracy in our AI-generated summaries, you acknowledge and understand that:

• Artificial intelligence can produce errors, hallucinations, misinterpretations, or biased conclusions.
• The Service analyzes abstracts only, not full-text papers. Important nuances, methodological details, and study limitations may be missed.
• Results should always be verified against the original source papers, which are linked in every analysis.
• The quality of results depends on the availability and relevance of PubMed abstracts for your specific query.
• AI models may have knowledge cutoffs and may not reflect the very latest research findings.
• MetaLens AI does not perform actual statistical meta-analysis — it provides AI-synthesized narrative summaries of research findings.
terms.s4Text:
You agree to use MetaLens AI only for lawful purposes and in accordance with these Terms. You agree NOT to:

• Use the Service for any illegal or unauthorized purpose.
• Attempt to gain unauthorized access to any part of the Service, its servers, or any systems connected to the Service.
• Use automated scripts, bots, scrapers, or other automated means to access the Service or extract data beyond normal usage.
• Interfere with or disrupt the Service or servers or networks connected to the Service.
• Attempt to reverse engineer, decompile, or disassemble any part of the Service.
• Use the Service to generate content that is misleading, fraudulent, or harmful.
• Resell, redistribute, or commercially exploit the Service or its outputs without prior written consent from SPINAI.
• Submit any personally identifiable patient information, protected health information (PHI), or confidential data through the Service.
• Exceed the usage limits established for your account tier (5 analyses per day for free tier users).
terms.s5Text:
MetaLens AI offers different service tiers:

• Free Tier: Available to all users without registration. Includes up to 5 analyses per day, 3-5 point summaries, and basic PubMed search capabilities. The free tier is supported by advertising.
• Pro Tier (Future): Will offer unlimited analyses, extended summaries, advanced filters, PDF export, and additional features for a monthly subscription fee.
• Team Tier (Future): Will offer all Pro features plus shared workspaces and team collaboration tools.

We reserve the right to modify, suspend, or discontinue any tier, feature, or pricing at any time with reasonable notice. Abuse of the free tier (including circumventing daily limits) may result in temporary or permanent suspension of access.
terms.s6Text:
The MetaLens AI name, logo, design, source code, and all associated trademarks and trade dress are the exclusive property of SPINAI. All rights reserved.

Research abstracts displayed through the Service are sourced from PubMed (NCBI/NIH) and are subject to their respective terms of use and copyright provisions. The AI-generated summaries are derivative works created by our AI synthesis engine and are provided for personal, non-commercial use.

You may share or cite the AI-generated summaries for personal, academic, or educational purposes, provided you acknowledge MetaLens AI as the source and include a link to the original PubMed papers.
terms.s7Text:
TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY APPLICABLE LAW, SPINAI AND ITS OFFICERS, DIRECTORS, EMPLOYEES, AGENTS, AND AFFILIATES SHALL NOT BE LIABLE FOR:

• Any direct, indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages arising from your use of or inability to use the Service.
• Any errors, inaccuracies, or omissions in the AI-generated content.
• Any medical decisions, clinical actions, or health outcomes based on information obtained from the Service.
• Any loss of data, business interruption, or financial loss resulting from your use of the Service.
• Any unauthorized access to or alteration of your transmissions or data.

Your sole remedy for dissatisfaction with the Service is to stop using it. In no event shall SPINAI's total liability exceed the amount you paid for the Service in the twelve (12) months preceding the claim, or fifty US dollars ($50), whichever is greater.
terms.s8Text: You agree to indemnify, defend, and hold harmless SPINAI, its officers, directors, employees, agents, and affiliates from and against any and all claims, damages, obligations, losses, liabilities, costs, or expenses (including reasonable attorneys' fees) arising from: (a) your use of and access to the Service; (b) your violation of any term of these Terms; (c) your violation of any third-party right, including any intellectual property or privacy right; or (d) any claim that your use of the Service caused damage to a third party.
terms.s9Text: MetaLens AI integrates with and contains links to third-party services including PubMed (NIH) and others. These third-party services have their own terms of service and privacy policies. SPINAI is not responsible for the content, privacy practices, or availability of any third-party services. Your interactions with third-party services are solely between you and the third party.
terms.s10Text: MetaLens AI displays advertisements provided by third-party advertising networks, including Google AdSense. These advertisements help support the free tier of our Service. By using MetaLens AI, you acknowledge that advertisements will be displayed alongside the Service's content. The presence of advertisements does not constitute an endorsement by SPINAI of the advertised products or services.
terms.s11Title: 11. Modifications to the Service and Terms
terms.s11Text: SPINAI reserves the right to modify, update, or discontinue the Service (or any part thereof) at any time without prior notice. We may also update these Terms from time to time. When we make material changes, we will update the "Last updated" date at the top of this page. Your continued use of the Service after any modifications to these Terms constitutes your acceptance of the revised Terms.
terms.s12Text: These Terms shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Republic of Korea, without regard to its conflict of law provisions. Any disputes arising from or relating to these Terms or the Service shall be resolved through good-faith negotiation. If negotiation fails, disputes shall be submitted to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts in Seoul, Republic of Korea.
terms.s13Text: If any provision of these Terms is found to be unenforceable or invalid by a court of competent jurisdiction, that provision shall be limited or eliminated to the minimum extent necessary so that these Terms shall otherwise remain in full force and effect.
terms.s14Text: If you have any questions about these Terms of Service, please contact us at:

pricing.faq2a: The free tier is available forever with 5 analyses per day. No credit card needed to start.
pricing.faq3q: What payment methods do you accept?
pricing.faq3a: We accept all major credit and debit cards through Stripe, our secure payment processor.
pricing.faq4a: Yes, you can upgrade or downgrade at any time. Changes take effect immediately and are prorated.

pricing.waitlistDesc: Be the first to access abstract generation, journal recommendations, and PDF export.
pricing.waitlistSuccess: You're on the list! We'll notify you when Pro is ready.
pricing.faq1aBeta: Yes! During beta, all features are free with no limits. When paid plans launch, the free tier will still include 5 analyses per day.
pricing.faq2aBeta: We are targeting Q2 2026. Sign up for the waitlist to be notified.

errors.noResults: No papers found for these keywords. Try different or broader terms.
errors.apiError: Something went wrong. Please try again.
errors.rateLimit: You've reached the daily limit. Upgrade to Pro for more analyses.
errors.networkError: Network error. Please check your connection and try again.
